data.title: Catena discontinues credit rating from Nordic Credit Rating
data.description:
Catena

The credit rating agreement Catena has had with Nordic Credit Rating (NCR) since 2021 has been terminated today.

28 February 2025 10.30 a.m. CEST

As part of Catena’s ongoing evaluation of the business, a decision has been made to terminate the agreement with NCR. Catena will continue to receive credit ratings from Fitch Ratings, which the company considers sufficient to meet the needs of the market and stakeholders.

“Over the years, we have established a high level of trust in the market through transparency, stable financial governance and a clear business strategy. This lays a solid foundation for continuing to attract investors and creditors while ensuring cost-effective information management”, comments Catena’s Chief Treasury Officer, David Silvesjö.

For further information, please contact:

David Silvesjö, Chief Treasury Officer, Tel. + 46 (0)730 70 22 22, david.silvesjo@catena.se

Jörgen Eriksson, CEO, Tel. +46 (0)730 70 22 42,  jorgen.eriksson@catena.se

Follow us: catena.se / LinkedIn

About Catena

Catena is a listed property company that sustainably develops and durably manages efficient logistics facilities through collaboration. Its strategically located properties supply the Scandinavian metropolitan areas and are adapted for both current and future flows of goods. The overarching objective is to generate a strong cash flow from operating activities to enable sustainable growth and stable returns. As of 31 December 2024, the properties had a total value of SEK 41,558 million. Catena’s shares are traded on NASDAQ Stockholm, Large Cap.

https://news.cision.com/catena/r/catena-discontinues-credit-rating-from-nordic-credit-rating%2Cc4112561

Catena discontinues credit rating from Nordic Credit Rating

data.title: Kepler Cheuvreux upgrades Saipem, sees diminished risks after Q4
data.description:
** Kepler Cheuvreux upgrades Saipem to "buy" from "hold" seeing fewer risks for the Italian energy contractor after it published Q4 results and reinstated dividend

** It also raises its PT on the stock by 13% to 3.40 euros

** While Saipem booked extra provisions for the quarter due to risks tied to projects in France and Thailand, its core profit was ahead of market and Kepler's expectations, which the broker calls "a big surprise"

** "Offshore activities continue to benefit from record visibility and high tendering activities. While risks remain, they have diminished post-publication," Kepler adds

** Out of 19 analysts that cover Saipem, 15 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy" and four rate it "hold"

data.title: Energoinvest, Turkey's Ares Trafo in talks on Bosnia transformer plant
data.description:
SARAJEVO (Bosnia and Herzegovina), February 28 (SeeNews) - Bosnia's majority state-owned energy infrastructure company Energoinvest [SAJ:ENIS] said it has started talks with Turkish transformer equipment maker Ares Trafo Ekipmanlari to establish an industrial transformer production plant in Bosnia.

The aim is to expand into European, African, and Middle Eastern markets, Energoinvest said in a press release on Thursday.

The factory would employ 250 people.

Via the strategic partnership, Energoinvest also wants to manufacture other components under its brand in Turkey.

Earlier this month, Energoinvest announced plans to open a new factory in Algeria together with a local partner, which would give the company a preferred supplier status in the country.

Energoinvest shares last traded on the Sarajevo bourse on February 27 at 3 marka ($1.6/1.53 euro), down 2.28%. Since the beginning of the year, Energoinvest's shares surged 172.7%.

(1 euro = 1.956 marka)

data.title: Finland's Valmet Bags Vessel System Order in China
data.description:
Valmet secured an order for its marine methanol fuel automation control system to be used in engineering services company Jan De Nul's two cable-laying vessels.

The order was placed by China Merchants Industry Digital Technology. It includes the application of methanol control and safety systems, communication ports as well as remote system access, the Finnish process technologies and automation company said Friday.

Valmet noted that the ships will be built at the China Merchants Heavy Industry shipyard. Financial details of the transaction were not disclosed.

data.title: Allianz Unit Faces Fine in Australia for Misleading Statements
data.description:
The Australian Securities and Investments Commission said Friday Allianz's Allianz Australia Insurance was fined AU$13.5 million for false or misleading statements about travel insurance policies.

The Supreme Court of New South Wales imposed the penalty after determining that between 2016 and 2018, Allianz violated the Corporations Act and misrepresented travel insurance coverage its domestic and international websites.

The court also fined AWP Australia AU$3.3 million for similar claims.

data.title: Wärtsilä to Build 350MW Energy Storage System for EnergyAustralia
data.description:
Wärtsilä was awarded a contract by EnergyAustralia to deliver a 350-megawatt energy storage system in Victoria, Australia, for renewable energy integration and grid stability.

The Finnish marine and energy technology group said Friday the scope of work includes engineering design, supply, commissioning, and a 15-year service agreement.

Construction is scheduled to start in 2025, with completion expected in 2027, ahead of the planned retirement of the Yallourn coal-fired power station in 2028. Once operational, the system will provide power to 230,000 homes for up to four hours at a time.

data.title: Deutsche Boerse's Clearstream deals with residual impact of ECB outage
data.description:
Deutsche Boerse's Clearstream said on Friday services were largely restored but that there was still some residual impact following an unprecedented outage in the European Central Bank's payment system.

The malfunction of the Target 2 system on Thursday, used to settle more than 3 trillion euros ($3.12 trillion) of daily payments and financial trades, meant transactions between banks could not go through.

The aftershocks are expected to continue for some days.

"We are in close contact with our clients to assess and to minimise impact wherever possible and are in regular contact with our regulators providing updates on the current situation", it said.

Clearstream accounts for around 500,000 transactions daily, equating to one million instructions and making up 54% of T2S volume.

($1 = 0.9614 euros)

data.title: Saudi Aramco settles March 2025 contract price for Propane
data.description: An industry source informed the Polymerupdate team that Saudi Aramco has settled its propane contract price for Asia, for the month of March 2025.The source said, "Saudi Aramco has settled its March contract price for propane at the USD 615/mt levels, a decline of USD 20/mt from its February 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: Saudi Aramco settles March 2025 contract price for Butane
data.description: An industry source informed the Polymerupdate team that Saudi Aramco has settled its butane contract price for Asia, for the month of March 2025.The source said, "Saudi Aramco has settled its March contract price for butane at the USD 605/mt levels, a drop of USD 20/mt from its February 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: Pearson 2024 Adjusted Earnings Increase, Revenue Decreases
data.description:
Pearson reported 2024 adjusted earnings Friday of 0.61 British pounds ($0.77) per diluted share, up from 0.58 pounds a year earlier.

Comparable analyst estimates were not readily available.

Sales for the year ended Dec. 31 were 3.55 billion pounds, down from 3.67 billion pounds a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected 3.51 billion pounds.
